data.title: Colgate-Palmolive Co. reports 1.4% increase in net sales for full year 2025, with GAAP EPS declining by 25% to $2.63.
data.description: Colgate-Palmolive Company reported a 1.4% increase in both full-year Net sales and Organic sales, despite a 0.7% negative impact from decreased private label pet volume. GAAP EPS decreased by 25% to $2.63 due to impairment charges linked to the skin health business, while Base Business EPS rose by 3% to $3.69. The company achieved a record net cash provided by operations of $4,198 for the year and returned $2.9 billion to shareholders through dividends and share buybacks.

data.title: LDO predicted to hit $0.75-$0.85 by Feb 2026, with short-term target at $0.52 and critical support at $0.44.
data.description: Summary of LDO Price Prediction:In the short term, LDO is expected to reach $0.52 within one week. The bullish breakout level is identified at $0.52, indicating potential for upward movement. On the downside, critical support for LDO is pegged at $0.44. This information comes from a crypto analyst report that delves into the future trajectory of LDO.

data.title: Liquid cooled EV charging cable market to reach $1.28 billion by 2032, growing from $0.51 billion in 2025 at 14% CAGR.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, a report by MarketsandMarkets™ predicts significant growth in the liquid cooled EV charging cable market. The market is expected to increase from $0.51 billion in 2025 to $1.28 billion by 2032, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 14.0% during the forecast period.

data.title: 2025 wildfire season by Cornea detailed with record human, economic impacts. 65,000+ wildfires burn effects on individuals and systems.
data.description: In New York, the 2025 wildfire season has brought significant changes in the management and financial burden of wildfire risk, as per a recent analysis by Cornea. The report highlights a shift in the trend of wildfires, which are no longer limited to a particular season or region. Winter fires have become catastrophic, leading to increased health issues due to smoke exposure, along with rising costs that are being passed on to consumers through utilities, insurance, and housing systems. The analysis reveals that over 65,000 wildfires have been recorded.

data.title: OTC Markets Group welcomes AltaGas Ltd. to OTCQX Best Market; 12,000 securities traded. AltaGas upgraded from Pink Limited.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, OTC Markets Group Inc. revealed that AltaGas Ltd., a prominent North American infrastructure firm, has met the requirements to trade on the OTCQX® Best Market. OTC Markets Group operates regulated markets for trading over 12,000 U.S. and international securities. AltaGas Ltd. (TSX: ALA; OTCQX: ATGFF) transitioned to the OTCQX from the Pink Limited™ Market.

data.title: Tonix Pharmaceuticals showcased Phase 3 RESILIENT data on TONMYA for fibromyalgia pain at Non-Opioid Pain Therapeutics Summit, showing notable pain reduction compared to placebo.
data.description: In the Phase 3 RESILIENT study, TONMYA showed a substantial decrease in fibromyalgia pain when compared to a placebo. The results displayed a significant improvement in pain levels, highlighting the effectiveness of TONMYA in managing fibromyalgia symptoms. This analysis underscores the potential of TONMYA in providing relief for individuals suffering from fibromyalgia, showcasing a promising advancement in treatment options for this condition. These findings offer hope to the estimated 10 million people in the United States alone who are affected by fibromyalgia, indicating a positive impact on managing their pain and overall quality of life.

data.title: KRAKacquisition Corp closes $345M IPO with 34.5M units sold at $10 each, including extra 4.5M from over-allotment option.
data.description: Cheyenne, Wyoming, USA - January 30, 2026 - KRAKacquisition Corp revealed the completion of its enlarged initial public offering of 34,500,000 units, with an extra 4,500,000 units sold through the full exercise of the underwriter's over-allotment option. Priced at $10.00 per unit, the offering generated $345 million in gross proceeds for the Company, exclusive of underwriting discounts, commissions, and other expenses related to the offering. The special purpose acquisition company is sponsored by affiliates of Natural Capital, Tribe Capital, and Payward, Inc.

data.title: Church & Dwight exceeded 2025 outlook with $6,203.2 million in net sales (1.6% increase) and 0.7% organic sales growth.
data.description: Ewing, New Jersey-based Church & Dwight Co., Inc. (NYSE: CHD) revealed that it has surpassed its expectations with robust sales and earnings growth, resulting in market share expansion across its worldwide portfolio. The company reported a 1.6% increase in full-year 2025 net sales, reaching $6,203.2 million, outperforming its projected 1.5% growth. Despite a 130 basis points decline from its discontinued VMS business and a slowdown in category growth, organic sales saw a 0.7% rise. This impressive performance showcases the company's resilience and adaptability in the market.

data.title: Brookfield Renewable reported strong 2025 results, with $1.33 billion FFO, a 10% increase year-over-year, and a 5% distribution increase.
data.description: Brookfield Renewable Partners reported financial results for the three and twelve months ended December 31, 2025, in US dollars. They had a net income attributable to unitholders of $410 million for three months and a net loss of $19 million for twelve months. Funds From Operations (FFO) for twelve months were $1,334 million, $2.01 per unit, up 10% year-over-year. They had strong operating performance, with hydro segment FFO at $607 million, a 19% YoY increase, and wind/solar segment FFO at $648 million. Brookfield committed $8.8 billion across strategic technologies in core markets and completed $4.5 billion in asset sales. They delivered a record ~8,000 megawatts of new capacity in 2025 globally and expect a run-rate of ~10,000 megawatts per year by 2027. They strengthened their balance sheet with $4.6 billion liquidity. Quarterly distribution was $0.392 per LP unit, payable on March 31, 2026, marking a 5% increase. Brookfield Renewable operates a large renewable power platform with hydroelectric, wind, solar, and storage facilities, and investments in sustainable solutions assets. They expect to deploy capital into growth opportunities and maintain BBB+ investment grade rating. More detailed financial statements are available on their website.

data.title: QuantumScape adds Geoff Ribar to board, a tech industry vet with CFO experience, joining as a director.
data.description: In California, QuantumScape Corporation (NASDAQ: QS), a prominent player in solid-state lithium-metal battery technology, has appointed Geoff Ribar to its board of directors. With a strong background in the technology sector, Ribar has served as CFO for various companies. Notably, he held the CFO position at Cadence Design Systems from 2010 to 2017. His appointment brings a wealth of experience to QuantumScape as they continue to advance in the industry.
